OWASP ZAP Scan Report

Target: http://192.168.1.49:3000

ZAP Version: 2.17.0

Mode: daemon

Spider: True

AJAX Spider: True

Active Scan: True

Total Alerts: 1841

Scan Timing

Total Duration: 5m 6s

  • ZAP Connection & Verification: 0.0s
  • Seed URL Access: 0.3s
  • Spider Scan: 20.0s
  • AJAX Spider Scan: 0.0s
  • Active Scan: 4m 46s
  • Passive Scan Queue Drain: 0.0s
  • Alert Collection: 0.5s

Warnings

Failed to disable robots.txt handling: Failed to parse JSON from http://127.0.0.1:8080/JSON/spider/action/setOptionHandleRobotsTxt/?apikey=&Boolean=false: HTTP Error 400: Bad Request
AJAX spider failed: Failed to parse JSON from http://127.0.0.1:8080/JSON/spiderAjax/action/scan/?apikey=&url=http%3A%2F%2F192.168.1.49%3A3000: HTTP Error 400: Bad Request

Alerts

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/api/Cards/:id

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/api/BasketItems/:id

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Application Error Disclosure

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/api/Recycles/:id

Evidence: HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error

Description: This page contains an error/warning message that may disclose sensitive information like the location of the file that produced the unhandled exception. This information can be used to launch further attacks against the web application. The alert could be a false positive if the error message is found inside a documentation page.

Solution: Review the source code of this page. Implement custom error pages. Consider implementing a mechanism to provide a unique error reference/identifier to the client (browser) while logging the details on the server side and not exposing them to the user.

Application Error Disclosure

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/profile

Evidence: HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error

Description: This page contains an error/warning message that may disclose sensitive information like the location of the file that produced the unhandled exception. This information can be used to launch further attacks against the web application. The alert could be a false positive if the error message is found inside a documentation page.

Solution: Review the source code of this page. Implement custom error pages. Consider implementing a mechanism to provide a unique error reference/identifier to the client (browser) while logging the details on the server side and not exposing them to the user.

Private IP Disclosure

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/profile

Evidence: 192.168.1.144

Description: A private IP (such as 10.x.x.x, 172.x.x.x, 192.168.x.x) or an Amazon EC2 private hostname (for example, ip-10-0-56-78) has been found in the HTTP response body. This information might be helpful for further attacks targeting internal systems.

Solution: Remove the private IP address from the HTTP response body. For comments, use JSP/ASP/PHP comment instead of HTML/JavaScript comment which can be seen by client browsers.

Timestamp Disclosure - Unix

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/metrics

Description: A timestamp was disclosed by the application/web server. - Unix

Solution: Manually confirm that the timestamp data is not sensitive, and that the data cannot be aggregated to disclose exploitable patterns.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/file-upload

Evidence:

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

X-Content-Type-Options Header Missing

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/metrics

Description: The Anti-MIME-Sniffing header X-Content-Type-Options was not set to 'nosniff'. This allows older versions of Internet Explorer and Chrome to perform MIME-sniffing on the response body, potentially causing the response body to be interpreted and displayed as a content type other than the declared content type. Current (early 2014) and legacy versions of Firefox will use the declared content type (if one is set), rather than performing MIME-sniffing.

Solution: Ensure that the application/web server sets the Content-Type header appropriately, and that it sets the X-Content-Type-Options header to 'nosniff' for all web pages. If possible, ensure that the end user uses a standards-compliant and modern web browser that does not perform MIME-sniffing at all, or that can be directed by the web application/web server to not perform MIME-sniffing.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/redirect

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/promotion

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/redirect

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Sub Resource Integrity Attribute Missing

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/promotion

Evidence:

Description: The integrity attribute is missing on a script or link tag served by an external server. The integrity tag prevents an attacker who have gained access to this server from injecting a malicious content.

Solution: Provide a valid integrity attribute to the tag.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/rest/continue-code-fixIt/apply/:continueCode

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/rest/continue-code-fixIt/apply/:continueCode

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/rest/chatbot/respond

Sub Resource Integrity Attribute Missing

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/promotion

Evidence:

Description: The integrity attribute is missing on a script or link tag served by an external server. The integrity tag prevents an attacker who have gained access to this server from injecting a malicious content.

Solution: Provide a valid integrity attribute to the tag.

Sub Resource Integrity Attribute Missing

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/promotion

Evidence:

Description: The integrity attribute is missing on a script or link tag served by an external server. The integrity tag prevents an attacker who have gained access to this server from injecting a malicious content.

Solution: Provide a valid integrity attribute to the tag.

Application Error Disclosure

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/rest/country-mapping

Evidence: HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error

Description: This page contains an error/warning message that may disclose sensitive information like the location of the file that produced the unhandled exception. This information can be used to launch further attacks against the web application. The alert could be a false positive if the error message is found inside a documentation page.

Solution: Review the source code of this page. Implement custom error pages. Consider implementing a mechanism to provide a unique error reference/identifier to the client (browser) while logging the details on the server side and not exposing them to the user.

Private IP Disclosure

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/rest/admin/application-configuration

Evidence: 192.168.99.100:3000

Description: A private IP (such as 10.x.x.x, 172.x.x.x, 192.168.x.x) or an Amazon EC2 private hostname (for example, ip-10-0-56-78) has been found in the HTTP response body. This information might be helpful for further attacks targeting internal systems.

Solution: Remove the private IP address from the HTTP response body. For comments, use JSP/ASP/PHP comment instead of HTML/JavaScript comment which can be seen by client browsers.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/sitemap.xml

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/sitemap.xml

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Modern Web Application

Risk: INFORMATIONAL | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/sitemap.xml

Evidence:

Description: The application appears to be a modern web application. If you need to explore it automatically then the Ajax Spider may well be more effective than the standard one.

Solution: This is an informational alert and so no changes are required.

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/b2b

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/profile/image

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/b2b

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Application Error Disclosure

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/rest/2fa

Evidence: HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error

Description: This page contains an error/warning message that may disclose sensitive information like the location of the file that produced the unhandled exception. This information can be used to launch further attacks against the web application. The alert could be a false positive if the error message is found inside a documentation page.

Solution: Review the source code of this page. Implement custom error pages. Consider implementing a mechanism to provide a unique error reference/identifier to the client (browser) while logging the details on the server side and not exposing them to the user.

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/routes/angular.js:42:18

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/routes/angular.js:42:18

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/morgan/index.js:144:5

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/morgan/index.js:144:5

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/morgan/index.js:144:5

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/routes/verify.js:111:5

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/profile/image/main.js

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/profile/assets/public/favicon_js.ico

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/profile/assets/public/favicon_js.ico

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/express/lib/router/index.js:421:3

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/express/lib/router/index.js:421:3

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/express/lib/router/index.js:421:3

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/express/lib/router/vendor.js

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/express/lib/router/vendor.js

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Low

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/profile/main.js

Evidence: 1981395349

Description: A timestamp was disclosed by the application/web server. - Unix

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/routes/main.js

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/routes/main.js

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/routes/main.js

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/morgan/main.js

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/morgan/main.js

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/morgan/main.js

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Description: A timestamp was disclosed by the application/web server. - Unix

Solution: Manually confirm that the timestamp data is not sensitive, and that the data cannot be aggregated to disclose exploitable patterns.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/profile/assets/public/styles.css

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/profile/assets/public/styles.css

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/profile/assets/public/styles.css

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/profile/assets/public/runtime.js

Evidence:

Description: The application appears to be a modern web application. If you need to explore it automatically then the Ajax Spider may well be more effective than the standard one.

Solution: This is an informational alert and so no changes are required.

Timestamp Disclosure - Unix

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Low

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/profile/assets/public/runtime.js

Solution: Manually confirm that the timestamp data is not sensitive, and that the data cannot be aggregated to disclose exploitable patterns.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/lib/polyfills.js

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/lib/polyfills.js

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/lib/polyfills.js

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/lib/polyfills.js

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/express/lib/router/assets/public/assets/public/favicon_js.ico

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/routes/assets/public/assets/public/favicon_js.ico

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/routes/assets/public/assets/public/favicon_js.ico

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/routes/assets/public/assets/public/favicon_js.ico

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/profile/image/assets/public/assets/public/styles.css

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/profile/image/assets/public/assets/public/styles.css

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/lib/assets/public/assets/public/favicon_js.ico

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/profile/assets/public/assets/public/polyfills.js

Evidence:

Description: The application appears to be a modern web application. If you need to explore it automatically then the Ajax Spider may well be more effective than the standard one.

Solution: This is an informational alert and so no changes are required.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/express/lib/router/assets/public/assets/public/runtime.js

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/express/lib/router/assets/public/assets/public/runtime.js

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/express/lib/router/assets/public/assets/public/runtime.js

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/routes/assets/public/assets/public/assets/public/favicon_js.ico

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/routes/assets/public/assets/public/assets/public/favicon_js.ico

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/routes/assets/public/assets/public/assets/public/favicon_js.ico

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/profile/image/assets/public/assets/public/assets/public/main.js

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/profile/image/assets/public/assets/public/assets/public/main.js

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/morgan/assets/public/assets/public/main.js

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/morgan/assets/public/assets/public/main.js

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/morgan/assets/public/assets/public/main.js

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/morgan/assets/public/assets/public/main.js

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/lib/assets/public/assets/public/main.js

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/lib/assets/public/assets/public/main.js

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/lib/assets/public/assets/public/main.js

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/routes/assets/public/assets/public/assets/public/assets/public/favicon_js.ico

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/routes/assets/public/assets/public/assets/public/styles.css

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/routes/assets/public/assets/public/assets/public/styles.css

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/routes/assets/public/assets/public/assets/public/styles.css

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/morgan/assets/public/assets/public/assets/public/styles.css

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/morgan/assets/public/assets/public/assets/public/styles.css

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/profile/assets/public/assets/public/assets/public/assets/public/runtime.js

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/profile/assets/public/assets/public/assets/public/assets/public/runtime.js

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/lib/assets/public/assets/public/assets/public/runtime.js

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/lib/assets/public/assets/public/assets/public/runtime.js

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/build/routes/fileServer.js:59:18

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

Evidence: 1981395349

Description: A timestamp was disclosed by the application/web server. - Unix

Solution: Manually confirm that the timestamp data is not sensitive, and that the data cannot be aggregated to disclose exploitable patterns.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/serve-index/main.js

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/serve-index/main.js

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion

Risk: LOW | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/serve-index/main.js

Evidence:

Description: The page includes one or more script files from a third-party domain.

Solution: Ensure JavaScript source files are loaded from only trusted sources, and the sources can't be controlled by end users of the application.

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/juice-shop/node_modules/serve-index/assets/public/vendor.js

Solution: Manually confirm that the timestamp data is not sensitive, and that the data cannot be aggregated to disclose exploitable patterns.

Cross-Domain Misconfiguration

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/ftp/quarantine/juicy_malware_linux_arm_64.url

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/ftp/quarantine

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: Medium

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/ftp/quarantine

Evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

Description: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.

Solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance). Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive set of domains, or remove all CORS headers entirely, to allow the web browser to enforce the Same Origin Policy (SOP) in a more restrictive manner.

Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

Risk: MEDIUM | Confidence: High

URL: http://192.168.1.49:3000/ftp/

Description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.

Solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

Risk: INFORMATIONAL | Confidence: Medium
